title: Monday onboarding kickoff - review initial document request list for new outsourced accounting client
description: Weekly recurring onboarding item. Before the Monday kickoff, review the draft document request list for completeness: bank access, prior close package, AP aging, open invoices, payroll reports, and tax deposit support. Flag any treasury access issues early, especially signatory evidence and user provisioning, so daily bookkeeping intake is not delayed in week one.

description: Narrative event affecting capacity planning. Following the senior accountant resignation during month-end close, update the Tuesday monthly close capacity board and reassign daily close blocker ownership for at-risk managed clients. Note any impact on reconciliation review cadence, AP processing coverage, and who will take partner-facing updates through the rest of April.

description: Add to Wednesday partner escalation list: several external-client returns remain not ready because source documents are outstanding. Document chase list should distinguish missing brokerage statements from unsigned organizers and note whether entity filings are also impacted. Include filing readiness comments for any clients that may slip if bank support or fixed-asset schedules are not received this week.

description: Audit support workstream for the retail client group still has stale PBC asks with no owner assigned. Review the oldest items, identify which schedules are client-dependent versus internal pull requests, and update the escalation tracker for the next partner huddle. Focus on requests tied to fixed assets, cash confirmations, and unsupported prepaid balances that are holding completion dates.

description: Weekly recurring trainee review loop for George McAdam and Daniel Jones. Include review notes on BL-32D277EF07F9 for 117000 Allowance for Doubtful Accounts in acme_cloud_FP-2026-04 so trainees practice tying clean support to certified reconciliations.

Review how invoice VEN-012-211225 from MetroShield Facility Services ($4,726.05, approved) and invoice VEN-014-300659 from Union Square Realty Partners ($6,379.51, approved) moved through approval and why they were not released within the expected pay cycle. Update handoff rules, exception thresholds, and invoice support requirements for managed clients with decentralized approvers.

title: Run expired W-9 sweep across managed-client vendor master
description:
Context
During triage of TimeLedger Nexus invoice VEN-010-693199 for $21,777.01 on acme_cloud, we confirmed the payment hold is tied to an expired vendor W-9 rather than an invoice-content issue. Daniel Jones directed that we log the broader follow-up so other vendors with stale tax documentation are not missed.

Acceptance criteria
- Pull a vendor-master report for managed clients showing W-9 expiration status.
- Identify all vendors with expired or missing W-9s.
- Prioritize any vendors tied to currently pending AP invoices.
- Propose a standard hard-deadline escalation path for conditioned payment release when a W-9 is stale.
- Return findings to AP ops with an owner list and next actions.

Related context
Current invoice under review: TimeLedger Nexus / VEN-010-693199 / $21,777.01 / entity acme_cloud.

title: Fix AP routing rule for departed approvers and sweep orphaned pending approvals
description:
Triggered by AP invoice apinv_5e09decd035d4443 for LatticeHill Learning Center (vendor invoice VEN-033-86573), which sat in pending_approval since 2025-06-30 for 334 days on account 210000 for $887.04 under the brookfield engagement. Review confirmed no vendor dispute, line memo matches the PO, and the approval route pointed to a user who left the firm in July 2025, so the invoice was never reassigned.

Andrea approved release in full on 2026-05-18.

Follow-up work:
- Patch the routing rule so departed approvers cannot retain AP approvals without reassignment.
- Sweep brookfield AP pending_approval invoices for additional orphan-approver cases, sorted by age.
- Deliver a count of impacted invoices by Friday 2026-05-22.
- Add a quarterly orphan-approver check to the AP workflow review cadence.

Acceptance criteria:
- Root-cause rule change is identified and implemented for departed-approver routing.
- Sweep output lists all brookfield pending_approval invoices with orphaned approvers and age ranking.
- Impact count is documented and shared by 2026-05-22.
- Recurring quarterly control check is defined and assigned.

title: Track conditioned release for apinv_9aa666fc03424902 and complete expired W-9 sweep
description:
Root cause: TimeLedger Nexus has an expired W-9 on file dated 2026-02-15, which is blocking payment release under firm policy.

Invoice in scope: apinv_9aa666fc03424902
Vendor invoice number: VEN-010-693199
Vendor: TimeLedger Nexus
Amount: $21,777.01
Entity: acme_cloud
GL account: 210000
Current SAP status: pending_approval

Conditioned release path:
- Hold release unless updated W-9 is received by the hard date set through partner sign-off.
- Brenda Abbas reported a soft vendor commitment to provide the refreshed W-9 by 2026-05-29.
- If the updated W-9 is not received by the agreed deadline, keep the invoice blocked and escalate per AP management direction.

Control follow-up:
- Mateo Kovac to run the broader vendor-master sweep for expired W-9s so this is not isolated to the TimeLedger Nexus record.
- Capture any additional vendors with stale tax documentation and route for remediation.

Acceptance criteria:
1. Conditioned release decision for apinv_9aa666fc03424902 is documented with hard-date requirement.
2. Brenda's 2026-05-29 soft commitment is recorded in the ticket.
3. Invoice status remains noted as pending_approval until documentation is refreshed and approval path is cleared.
4. Mateo owns the vendor-master expired W-9 sweep follow-up and reports findings.

title: Verify GraniteRack AP void-and-rebill for VEN-012-753165 / apinv_6131b7c637aa4b6e
description:
Track and verify the GraniteRack void-and-rebill path for invoice VEN-012-753165 tied to AP record apinv_6131b7c637aa4b6e.

Context:
- Invoice has been pending_approval since 2026-03-07 and is 85 days old as of universe cutoff, past internal AP approval target.
- Ben confirmed the invoice references superseded SOW-2024-GR-rev3 instead of active SOW-2025-GR-rev1 effective 2026-01-15. Tariq pulled the SOW reference off the invoice line.
- Ben also confirmed the underlying compute obligation was accrued on account 219000.
- Brenda advised GraniteRack AR can void and reissue in the next billing cycle with target reissue date 2026-06-05.
- Brenda flagged that the GraniteRack vendor-master description still points to the deprecated SOW and may create repeat issues across entities; Lena Park to own the procurement-side cleanup.
- Priya Khatri confirmed void-and-rebill is the correct AP disposition policy call.

Verification checklist:
1. Confirm current status of apinv_6131b7c637aa4b6e remains pending_approval and no one approved or paid VEN-012-753165.
2. Confirm supporting contract mismatch is SOW-2024-GR-rev3 versus SOW-2025-GR-rev1 and document whether line rates and milestones differ.
3. Confirm accrual support on account 219000 covers the obligation period referenced by the invoice.
4. Follow up on GraniteRack AR reissue due 2026-06-05 and capture replacement invoice details once received.
5. Validate whether GraniteRack vendor-master description needs correction for acme_cloud and any other affected entities, coordinating with Lena Park.

Sources already opened in prior thread/email traffic; use this issue as the single tracking record for disposition and follow-through.

title: AP escalation: TimeLedger Nexus VEN-010-514242 / apinv_d3019cdcc6ed44b2
description:
Invoice apinv_d3019cdcc6ed44b2 for TimeLedger Nexus invoice VEN-010-514242 remains in pending_approval status for acme_cloud. Invoice_date is 2026-03-05 and the item is now 87 days old, past our 60-day aged-exception threshold.

Amount summary:
- Total invoice amount: $24,475.25
- Undisputed portion: $17,825.00
- Disputed portion: $6,650.25 tied to the Phase-3 post-go-live tuning milestone

Context gathered from internal review and vendor follow-up:
- Prior file trail indicates the invoice stalled around a Phase-3 deliverable dispute tied to full SOW billing.
- Credit-memo follow-up from early April 2026 did not resolve; vendor confirmed the credit memo never arrived.
- Prior vendor AR contact left TimeLedger Nexus in late January 2026 (roughly five weeks before the invoice was issued), and the issue has been reopened with a new AR lead.

Recommended disposition:
- Release payment for the undisputed $17,825.00
- Hold the disputed $6,650.25 pending receipt of the credit memo or controller-side reaffirmation of the dispute status

Policy follow-up:
- Rerun the 90-day SLA scan across acme_cloud AP items still in pending_approval status to identify any additional aged exceptions requiring escalation.

Acceptance criteria:
- Disposition owner assigned and split-payment path tracked to completion
- Vendor credit-memo status documented
- 90-day SLA scan rerun for acme_cloud pending_approval AP items and exceptions logged as needed

title: Track Pinecrest vendor dispute and complete license-utilization audit

- Prepare dispute-tracking notes and next-step owner once approval is received.

title: Track GraniteRack void-and-rebill and vendor-master sweep
description:
Context
Daniel Jones directed AP to pursue void-and-rebill for GraniteRack Compute Services invoice VEN-012-753165 for $39,090.56 because the invoice references deprecated SOW-2024-GR-rev3 and cannot be paid as submitted, even though the underlying compute obligation is real and was accrued to account 219000. Brenda Abbas confirmed GraniteRack AR can void and reissue under SOW-2025-GR-rev1 in the next billing cycle targeting 2026-06-05, and also flagged that the GraniteRack vendor-master description may still reference the deprecated SOW. Lena Park confirmed from procurement that the supersession went effective 2026-01-15, so the vendor-master entry is stale rather than ambiguous. Priya Khatri signed off on void-and-rebill as the AP-policy disposition.

Scope
Track the rebill follow-up for the original AP exception tied to apinv_6131b7c637aa4b6e and document a sweep of GraniteRack vendor-master descriptions across managed entities to identify any lingering SOW-2024-GR-rev3 references.

Acceptance criteria
- Confirm owner and expected date for GraniteRack void and reissue of VEN-012-753165 under SOW-2025-GR-rev1.
- Record the original invoice amount of $39,090.56 and link this work back to the AP exception context (85 days in pending_approval as of universe cutoff).
- Inventory Brookfield GraniteRack vendor-master description references that still cite SOW-2024-GR-rev3, with Lena Park owning the procurement-side cleanup.
- Note any additional entities or recurring invoice risk discovered during the sweep.
- Keep this as a tracking breadcrumb only; no accounting-system write or approval action is taken from this issue.

Requested by Daniel Jones; Owen Mercer to open and coordinate, Lena Park on vendor-master cleanup.

title: Track Pinecrest VEN-006-193120 dispute for invoice apinv_dff20c11abdc495c
description:
Daniel asked that this be opened for dispute tracking and broader Pinecrest license review.

Invoice references
- AP invoice id: apinv_dff20c11abdc495c
- Vendor: Pinecrest Workflow Works
- Vendor invoice number: VEN-006-193120
- Client/entity context: brookfield
- Amount under review: $1,040.63

Contract decomposition supporting the billed amount
- Base contract: 5 seats at $208.12 per month
- Per-seat add: $208.13 per month
- Disputed billed amount of $1,040.63 matches 5 months of 1 additional seat billed for March through July 2025

Audit findings
- IT provisioning review found no SSO grants for any additional Pinecrest seat during 2025
- Usage telemetry showed 5 active users across the full billed period, not 6
- Evidence supports that the billed incremental seat was not provisioned and not used

Recommended disposition
- Escalate to vendor dispute
- Maintain payment hold on invoice apinv_dff20c11abdc495c pending vendor response and credit/rebill outcome
- Treat this as a service-delivery dispute rather than an internal approval-delay issue

Follow-up required
- Complete a base-seat utilization audit for Pinecrest with due date 2026-06-12 to confirm whether the contracted 5-seat baseline remains appropriate going forward

Note
- The Airtable vendor-dispute tracker will be appended outside this scenario.

title: Review CrownPeak AP queue and tune duplicate detection for memo variance
description:
Trigger invoice for northstar_legal: apinv_e0235997b4964a8f, CrownPeak Cyber Defense invoice VEN-030-353041 for $459.84, has been sitting pending approval since 2026-03-09 (83 days old as of the weekly exceptions scan) and appears to be a duplicate of already paid sibling invoice VEN-030-353009, paid on 2026-03-13 for the same March 2026 MSSP cycle. The sibling was paid four days after VEN-030-353041 was issued, indicating CrownPeak AR caught the duplicate but did not void the reissue cleanly on their side.

Vendor-side confirmation relayed internally indicates VEN-030-353041 was a billing-system reissue generated during CrownPeak's February 2026 platform migration (roughly three weeks before the duplicate was issued) and VEN-030-353009 was the valid invoice. No additional March billing is expected.

Control gap observed: duplicate detection did not catch the sibling invoices because memo text differed materially enough to evade the current match logic. One memo reads "Mar MSSP retainer" and the other reads "March MSSP monthly fee." This should be reviewed as a workflow/control issue, not only a one-off duplicate dismissal. Tariq's parallel-history pull on the CrownPeak vendor file backs up the duplicate finding.

Also review the two other long-pending CrownPeak items in the northstar_legal AP queue called out in the weekly exceptions review. Brenda's quick pass suggests they do not look like obvious duplicates, so they need separate aging and approval-path review.

Acceptance criteria:
- Document the duplicate relationship between apinv_e0235997b4964a8f / VEN-030-353041 and paid sibling VEN-030-353009.
- Propose a duplicate-detection rule adjustment or fuzzy-match threshold change for memo-variance cases like "Mar MSSP retainer" vs "March MSSP monthly fee" for Priya to sign off on as rule owner.
- Review the two other long-pending CrownPeak invoices for northstar_legal and summarize whether they are approval bottlenecks, coding issues, or separate vendor follow-ups.
- Capture recommended AP queue cleanup steps for CrownPeak items so the same pattern is easier to detect going forward.

Related context from internal escalation is preserved in email thread email_scen_033_ap_escalation_0003 and vendor confirmation email_scen_033_ap_escalation_0004.

title: Add partner sign-off conditions to CrownPeak duplicate-detection follow-up
description:
Matthew Li approved a narrative dismissal for apinv_e0235997b4964a8f based on Brenda Abbas's vendor confirmation that VEN-030-353041 was a February 2026 CrownPeak platform-migration reissue and the matched paid sibling invoice ID VEN-030-353009 (paid 2026-03-13 for the March 2026 MSSP cycle).

Scope added by partner:
- Test the fuzzy-match duplicate-detection rule across all three entities covered by the AP workflow standardization effort, with Priya Khatri owning the final rule decision before any production change.
- Tariq Soto to manually review the two other long-pending CrownPeak items for northstar_legal before the next exceptions scan.
- Keep this as narrative approval only; apinv_e0235997b4964a8f remains pending_approval in SAP and no actual void is to be executed in this scenario.

Use this issue to document test results, manual review conclusions, and any rule-tuning recommendation before the next scan cycle.

title: Finalize Pinecrest dispute notice conditions after partner approval

- cite telemetry seat counts showing 5 active users rather than 6 across the billed period
- give Pinecrest a 30-day response window ending 2026-06-21
- log the brookfield vendor-dispute tracker the same day outside this scenario

title: Attach CrownPeak coding-classification clarification to follow-up issue trail
description:
Add the AP review conclusion for CrownPeak invoice VEN-030-817856 tied to apinv_178dc009be6c474f to the existing follow-up issue trail for northstar_legal.

Context:
- Daniel Jones reviewed Owen Mercer's duplicate-check write-up and Ben Arinzo's scope-letter classification confirmation.

Acceptance criteria:

title: Document CrownPeak IR coding-helper rule from manual review
description:
Comment on issue_dfa400bcc1314b07a2c85aeaea3bf746:

Adding the classification breadcrumb from the manual review on northstar_legal CrownPeak invoice apinv_178dc009be6c474f (vendor invoice VEN-030-817856, $1,152.41).

Findings preserved here:
- Not a duplicate. Vendor/amount/billing-period review did not find a paid sibling, and the memo tied the charge to the July 2025 IR engagement.
- The long hold came from coding to 210000 instead of 219000.
- Recommendation from review remains re-code to 219000 and release.

Process-control follow-up:
The AP coding-helper should suggest 219000 when invoice memo text includes IR or incident response tokens for CrownPeak and similar MSSP vendors, so these incident-response add-on bills do not get routed to 210000 by default.

This note is documentation only and does not change SAP, Oracle GL, BlackLine, or Records Vault state.

title: Managing partner update - major multi-service client win requires tax and compliance readiness checklist
description: Narrative event follow-up. Managing partner announced a major multi-service client win spanning outsourced accounting, tax, and compliance. Build a readiness checklist covering onboarding timing, tax document intake, annual report calendar setup, audit-support expectations, and staffing constraints through 2026-05 month-end. Include immediate asks for entity registration details and document-retention preferences so work can launch cleanly.

title: Append VEN-030-736427 stale-void write-up to existing CrownPeak follow-up record
description:
Add the closeout summary for CrownPeak invoice VEN-030-736427 / apinv_e42396e9bdd64294 to the existing combined CrownPeak follow-up record so the three-of-three arc is documented in one place.

Context:
- Manual review concluded this is not a duplicate or live billing dispute.
- The underlying Q1 2026 IR contingency retainer for northstar_legal was cancelled by mutual agreement on 2026-02-28 when coverage moved in-house.
- Pre-bill was actually issued on 2026-03-11, about eleven days after the cancellation was logged, and is now 81 days old.
- Recommended disposition is void as stale on Brookfield's side.
- CrownPeak AR confirmed a manual credit memo will be backfilled within five business days.
- Daniel Jones is taking the partner follow-up to Matthew Li; Priya Khatri is drafting the AP-side policy framing on cancellation-triggered void scans.

Acceptance criteria:
- Append the narrative disposition and source breadcrumbs from Slack/email to the existing CrownPeak follow-up record.
- Note that this closes the third of three CrownPeak items.
- Keep all three CrownPeak follow-ups consolidated on one record for future traceability.

title: Close out CrownPeak three-item follow-up arc on existing record
description:
Append closeout note to existing CrownPeak follow-up issue.

Third item summary: apinv_e42396e9bdd64294 / VEN-030-736427 for $422.32 on northstar_legal account 219000 is not a duplicate. Manual review tied the line memo to a Q1 2026 IR contingency retainer pre-bill that was cancelled by mutual agreement on 2026-02-28 when northstar_legal moved IR coverage in-house. The pre-bill was issued on 2026-03-11 (now 81 days old in pending_approval) about eleven days after the cancellation was already on record, and it stayed in pending_approval because the original pre-bill was never voided and no credit memo was issued at cancellation.

Cross-scenario consolidation for the three CrownPeak invoices: this existing issue is the single breadcrumb for the full follow-through arc, including Owen's stale-void recommendation, Brenda's confirmation that CrownPeak AR has the cancellation on record and will issue a manual credit memo, Tariq's line-level detail pull, and Daniel's direction to treat the item as stale and close the three-of-three queue here.

Process note from Anaya Wallace, with Priya Khatri owning the AP-side policy framing: add a recurring cross-check between vendor cancellation events and AP invoices still sitting in pending_approval so cancelled retainers and similar pre-bills are surfaced before they age. Priya's read is that this should be a cancellation-event triggered AP void scan, not a periodic sweep, so a cancellation logged on the vendor side immediately fires an AP-side look at any open pre-bills for that engagement.
